body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: partial

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes

The token-accounting primitive is *token reporting*: per-turn token counts (input, output, cache read/write) in a field the parser can extract, with `headless_mode` (`replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` → `headless_mode: true`). Aider satisfies both halves of the scenario at the agent layer:

1. **Per-turn counts that accumulate.** After every model call aider prints `Tokens: <sent> sent, <received> received.` and persists a `> Tokens: …` blockquote to `.aider.chat.history.md` in the project CWD (https://aider.chat/docs/faq.html). Running three back-to-back one-message turns yields three such lines, each carrying that call's real usage — exactly the multi-turn accumulator the scenario locks down.
2. **Cache buckets.** With `--cache-prompts` (and `--no-stream`, since the provider APIs don't surface cache stats while streaming — https://aider.chat/docs/usage/caching.html), aider additionally reports cache segments. `calculate_and_show_tokens_and_cost` in `aider/coders/base_coder.py` builds the report conditionally: `"Tokens: {sent} sent"`, then `", {cache_write} cache write"` if `cache_write_tokens`, then `", {cache_hit} cache hit"` if `cache_hit_tokens`, then `", {received} received."`. So a fully-cached turn shows e.g. `Tokens: 21k sent, 19k cache hit, 964 received` (https://github.com/Aider-AI/aider/blob/main/aider/coders/base_coder.py). The cache numbers exist.

### irrlicht_observes: partial

The aider adapter is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/agent.go`). Judging the User-observable signals against the parser as it exists today splits cleanly into an observable half and a blind half.

**Observable — the multi-turn accumulator (the scenario's primary assertion):**

- `TotalTokens > 0` after turn 1, non-decreasing after turn 2 — **yes**. Each `> Tokens:` line is matched by `tokensRE` and routed through `closeModelCall` (`parser.go`), which emits an `assistant_message` carrying a `PerTurnContribution{Usage: {Input: sent, Output: received}}` and a `TokenSnapshot{Input, Output, Total: sent+received}`. `applyTokenSnapshot`/the cumulative accumulator in `core/pkg/tailer/tailer_metrics.go` sums per-turn contributions, so across three turns `TotalTokens` rises monotonically. `parser_test.go` (`TestParser_MultipleTokensLines`, lines 418-451; the multi-`> Tokens:` cases) pin one `assistant_message` per `> Tokens:` line across consecutive turns. The three-no-op-turn arc and the non-decreasing-TotalTokens assertion are fully met.

**Blind — the cache distinction (`CacheReadTokens > 0`; fresh input = 0 for a fully-cached turn):**

- **no event would prove this.** Two independent reasons:
  1. **Transport gap.** Aider's cache-bucket report is emitted via `self.io.tool_output(self.usage_report)` — terminal only; `show_usage_report` is never appended to chat history. Every `> Tokens:` line that lands in `.aider.chat.history.md` is the bare `N sent, M received.` form. The 11 distinct `> Tokens:` lines across irrlicht's own recorded aider transcripts confirm this — not one carries a `cache hit`/`cache write` segment.
  2. **Parser gap.** Even if a cache segment did reach the markdown, `tokensRE` (`^>\s*Tokens:\s*([\d.]+\s*[kKmM]?)\s*sent,\s*([\d.]+\s*[kKmM]?)\s*received(.*)$`) captures only `sent` and `received`; the `cache write`/`cache hit` text would fall into the `(.*)$` rest group, which `closeModelCall` scans only for a `$… message` cost suffix. `UsageBreakdown.CacheRead`/`CacheCreation` are never populated, so `metrics.CacheReadTokens` (`tailer.go`, `tailer_metrics.go`) stays 0. The "`CacheReadTokens > 0`" signal can never fire, and "fresh input tokens = 0 for a fully-cached turn" is unobservable because aider reports cache-hit tokens *separately* from `sent` — a cached turn still shows non-zero `sent`.

The scenario's core multi-turn accumulator is observed accurately; the cache-bucket sub-assertion is structurally unobservable through aider's current transport. That is a concrete carve-out on a real signal, so the honest verdict is `partial` (not `yes`-with-caveat): one of the named User-observable signals — the cache distinction — cannot be produced at all.

## Matrix

Ratifies the existing coverage cell (`agent-scenarios-coverage.json` → aider/token-accounting: `yes`/`partial`, "regex-parsed '> Tokens: N sent, M received'; no cache-bucket distinction"). No drift.

Primitive → capability key: the primitive is *token reporting*; the matching key in `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` is `cost_tracking: false`, which is consistent — aider carries sent/received token counts but no per-bucket cache accounting or reliable provider cost into the transcript.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: aider's cache-bucket usage report (`Tokens: … cache write … cache hit …`) is written via io.tool_output to the terminal only — it is never appended to .aider.chat.history.md, so irrlicht's file-watching transport never sees it. The multi-turn token accumulator is fully observed; the cache distinction is not.

Even if a cache segment reached the markdown, the parser's tokensRE captures only `sent`/`received`; closeModelCall never sets UsageBreakdown.CacheRead/CacheCreation, so CacheReadTokens stays 0. Closing this would need both a transcript-level cache line AND a regex/parser change.

aider's cache-hit tokens are reported separately from `sent` (sent stays non-zero on a cached turn), so the scenario's 'fresh input tokens = 0 for a fully-cached turn' assertion does not map onto aider's reporting shape even at the agent layer.

sources.note: --cache-prompts enables provider prompt caching; cache statistics/costs unavailable while streaming, so --no-stream is required to surface them

calculate_and_show_tokens_and_cost builds 'Tokens: {sent} sent[, {cache_write} cache write][, {cache_hit} cache hit], {received} received.'; show_usage_report emits it via io.tool_output (terminal) — NOT append_chat_history
aider writes per-project chat history to .aider.chat.history.md including the `> Tokens:` blockquote lines
Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} — irrlicht observes only what lands in the markdown history, not terminal output

tokensRE captures sent/received only (cache text falls in the unused rest group); closeModelCall sets UsageBreakdown.Input/Output + TokenSnapshot{Input,Output,Total} but never CacheRead/CacheCreation; one assistant_message per `> Tokens:`
accumulates per-turn contributions into cumulative TotalTokens (monotonic across turns); CumCacheReadTokens sourced from UsageBreakdown.CacheRead, which aider never populates → stays 0
TokenSnapshot/UsageBreakdown carry CacheRead+CacheCreation fields (lines 223-237); they exist but the aider parser leaves them zero
multi-`> Tokens:` cases pin one assistant_message per token line and monotonic accumulation across turns; every fixture uses the bare `N sent, M received.` form — no cache segment
headless_mode: true (primitive's required capability); cost_tracking: false — token counts present, no per-bucket cache accounting in the transcript
